_comment: Public authority team names for O27, category 4. These are state-sponsored or institutional teams (police, military, railways, customs, postal, ports authorities). The pattern is invariably <City/State> <Authority> with no mascot — institutions don't need cute names. Real-world precedent: Indian Railways cricket team, Mumbai Police, Pakistan Railways, Brazilian state police football clubs, the various military sports teams across South Asia and Africa. The category is intentionally rare (~6-10 teams across the universe). Regional weighting reflects where the institutional-sports tradition actually exists.

authority_types._comment: The base authority pool. Each entry has 'regions' listing which regional locales the type fits, since not every institution exists in every region's sports culture.

law_enforcement._real_precedent: Mumbai Police, Karachi Police, Sao Paulo PM

military._real_precedent: Service teams across South Asia and Africa

transport_infrastructure._real_precedent: Indian Railways cricket team is the canonical example

national_services._real_precedent: State Bank of India sports teams

naming_pattern._comment: Pattern is always <City or Province> <Authority>, no modifier, no suffix, no mascot. The austerity of the naming is the point.

regional_distribution._comment: Suggested per-league authority team counts in the 84-team universe. Heaviest in Subcontinent+ME (where the tradition is strongest in real cricket), lighter elsewhere, absent in some.

americas_premier._note: Maybe Sao Paulo PM or Caracas Municipal

rising_sun._note: Japanese/Korean corporate sports culture is non-institutional; skip

europa._note: European football is club-tradition; skip or one rare exception

pacifica._note: Sydney Defence Force or Manila Police

indo_malay._note: Jakarta Police or Kuala Lumpur Customs

subcontinent._note: Mumbai Police, Indian Railways, Pakistan Army-equivalent. Maximum representation; this is the home of the tradition

african_federation._note: African Federation uses its own real club-naming conventions (see african_federation_teams.json). Not the institutional/state-sports pattern that exists in South Asian cricket.
